channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [7] All-optical control of antiferromagnetic domains via an inverse optical magnetoelectric effect
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Y. Tokura, N. Ogawa

Authors: S. Toyoda, V. Kocsis, Y. Tokunaga, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Antiferromagnetic domain control relevant to magnetic quantum materials and potential TRSB.

Tags: #antiferromagnetism #magneticmaterials

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02608-4 All-optical writing of antiferromagnetic domains using the inverse optical magnetoelectric effect in ferrotoroidic LiNiPO4 is demonstrated.
item.dc:title: [7] All-optical control of antiferromagnetic domains via an inverse optical magnetoelectric effect
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Y. Tokura, N. Ogawa; Authors: S. Toyoda, V. Kocsis, Y. Tokunaga, et al.
V. Kocsis
Y. Tokunaga
I. Kézsmárki
Y. Taguchi
T. Arima
Y. Tokura
N. Ogawa
item.description:
Last authors: Y. Tokura, N. Ogawa

Authors: S. Toyoda, V. Kocsis, Y. Tokunaga, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Antiferromagnetic domain control relevant to magnetic quantum materials and potential TRSB.

Tags: #antiferromagnetism #magneticmaterials

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02608-4 All-optical writing of antiferromagnetic domains using the inverse optical magnetoelectric effect in ferrotoroidic LiNiPO4 is demonstrated.

item.title: [8] Detecting linear dichroism with atomic resolution
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez

Authors: Roger Guzman, Ján Rusz, Ang Li, Juan Carlos Idrobo, Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: New atomic-resolution method for orbital occupation/electronic structure, highly relevant to user's interests.

Tags: #spectroscopy #methodology #electronicstructure #EELS

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02606-6 An electron linear dichroism method uses electron energy loss spectroscopy with an atomic-sized probe and momentum transfer selection along two orthogonal directions to resolve orbital occupation at individual atomic columns in real space.
item.dc:title: [8] Detecting linear dichroism with atomic resolution
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez; Authors: Roger Guzman, Ján Rusz, Ang Li, Juan Carlos Idrobo, Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez
Ján Rusz
Ang Li
Juan Carlos Idrobo
Wu Zhou
Jaume Gazquez
item.description:
Last authors: Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez

Authors: Roger Guzman, Ján Rusz, Ang Li, Juan Carlos Idrobo, Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: New atomic-resolution method for orbital occupation/electronic structure, highly relevant to user's interests.

Tags: #spectroscopy #methodology #electronicstructure #EELS

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02606-6 An electron linear dichroism method uses electron energy loss spectroscopy with an atomic-sized probe and momentum transfer selection along two orthogonal directions to resolve orbital occupation at individual atomic columns in real space.

item.title: [8] Two-dimensional magnets stack up against multiferroic competition
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Meixin Cheng, Adam W. Tsen

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: 2D magnet with TRSB and magnetoelectric coupling, relevant to quantum materials and user's interests.

Tags: #2Dmaterials #magnetism #time-reversalsymmetrybreaking #multiferroics

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02591-w Bilayer 1T-CrTe2 breaks time-reversal and inversion symmetry in its magnetic state, allowing for strong magnetoelectric coupling at room temperature.
item.dc:title: [8] Two-dimensional magnets stack up against multiferroic competition
item.dc:creator: Meixin Cheng, Adam W. Tsen
Adam W. Tsen
item.description:
Last authors: Meixin Cheng, Adam W. Tsen

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: 2D magnet with TRSB and magnetoelectric coupling, relevant to quantum materials and user's interests.

Tags: #2Dmaterials #magnetism #time-reversalsymmetrybreaking #multiferroics

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02591-w Bilayer 1T-CrTe2 breaks time-reversal and inversion symmetry in its magnetic state, allowing for strong magnetoelectric coupling at room temperature.
